decomp_spread_map.note: lambda = how far land's re-benched deeper spine q[2:] recovers from the measured declining ladder toward the rho-optimal rising ladder, with depth-1 and branch-width at rho-opt. lambda below 0.90 => sub-500 even with width fully restored => a SPREAD failure.

forward_map_inverse.note: land #71 feeds its measured q[2..9] -> implied lambda_hat_built -> read predicted_lcb_tps off forward_map.

handoff_lines.fern_179_packet: BUILD LINE (denken #183, margin-aware λ-acceptance card): land #71's kernel must demonstrate per-depth self-KV recovery λ ≥ 0.9052 (both-bugs, finite-sample-LCB-clears-500 bar — wirbel #175's ±10.9 ⊕ kanna #159's σ_hw=4.86), STRICTER than #178's 0.8384 central point estimate by Δλ=+0.0668; the measured q[2..9] ladder is tested against the per-depth card (depth 7 most binding); the one pre-build measured point λ̂=0.342 (liveprobe) is far below.
handoff_lines.land_71_calculator: land #71 acceptance: hit per-depth q[2..9] ≥ [0.7363, 0.7724, 0.8050, 0.8196, 0.8200, 0.8254, 0.8254, 0.8254] (both-bugs, at the LCB bar λ*=0.9052); depth 7 is the hardest target. Forward calculator: feed your measured q[2..9] → implied λ̂_built → read predicted LCB-TPS; clear iff predicted-LCB ≥ 500. (At the unbuilt λ̂=0.342: LCB≈404, a clear miss.)

per_depth_acceptance_ladder.note: q[2..9] = self-KV-governed depths (depth-1 is the separate BUG-1 axis). Spine parameterizes depths 1-7; depths 8-9 are flat-extrapolated (== depth-7 value), exactly as the imported E[T] DP's qd_at clamps.

synthesis.verdict: MARGIN-AWARE-CARD-STAMPED. The finite-sample-LCB build bar is STRICTER than #178's point estimate: both-bugs must demonstrate per-depth self-KV recovery lambda >= 0.9052 (vs #178's 0.8384 central point estimate; +0.0668 more recovery for the 95% LCB to clear 500), descent-only lambda >= 0.9750. The one pre-build measured point lambda_hat=0.342 (liveprobe) is far below both bars. land #71's measured q[2..9] ladder is tested against the per-depth card; the forward map converts any measured ladder to a predicted LCB-TPS go/no-go. NOT a launch.
